key_insight: MOSAIC is a two-phase multi-LLM system that can synthesize EHR evidence into severity phenotypes and, in type 2 diabetes, it captured dimensions beyond standard rule-based scores.
clinical_relevance: It may help produce richer, more clinically meaningful severity stratification from structured records, but it remains a research framework rather than a deployable clinical tool.
method: Agentic two-phase LLM workflow for severity phenotyping
Compared open-weight vs proprietary pipelines on SyntheticMass EHR data
Benchmarked against three rule-based ground truths and outcome associations
evidence_signal: On SyntheticMass, open-weight MOSAIC closely matched the proprietary version (weighted kappa 0.773) and showed moderate-to-fair agreement with established severity scores while also separating mortality and complication risk.
watch_item: Performance was evaluated mainly in synthetic type 2 diabetes data, so external validation on real-world EHRs and across diseases is still needed.
paper_title: Multi-Large Language Model Orchestrated Severity Assessment of Clinical Records (MOSAIC)
